title: Podman Create: podman system check - layer content modified. Podman reports "damaged" layers, image, and containers
body:
### Issue Description

I'm filing this issue here as it appears that it is not due to distrobox, where I originally filed it. https://github.com/89luca89/distrobox/issues/1656

In short, it seems that creating a container with `podman create` with the incantation distrobox uses leads to some sort of mtime mismatch, which `podman system check` considers to be "damage". Even after removing the container, the damaged moniker persists.

The container still runs fine. So far, I have not seen any ill effects. This occurs with any container image I've tried, alpine is just small so more convenient.

Removing both the container and image clears the errors from `podman system check`.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Fresh Fedora 41 Server install from iso into VM. 

Before running this command, `podman system check` does not report errors.

```
podman create \
--hostname "framework13" \
--name "alpine" \
--privileged \
--security-opt label=disable \
--security-opt apparmor=unconfined \
--pids-limit=-1 \
--user root:root \
--ipc host \
--network host \
--pid host \
--label "manager=distrobox" \
--label "distrobox.unshare_groups=0" \
--env "SHELL=bash" \
--env "HOME=/home/ben" \
--env "container=podman" \
--env "TERMINFO_DIRS=/usr/share/terminfo:/run/host/usr/share/terminfo" \
--env "CONTAINER_ID=alpine" \
--volume /tmp:/tmp:rslave \
--volume "/usr/bin/distrobox-init":/usr/bin/entrypoint:ro \
--volume "/usr/bin/distrobox-export":/usr/bin/distrobox-export:ro \
--volume "/usr/bin/distrobox-host-exec":/usr/bin/distrobox-host-exec:ro \
--volume "/home/ben":"/home/ben":rslave \
--volume /:/run/host/:rslave \
--volume /dev:/dev:rslave \
--volume /sys:/sys:rslave \
--volume /dev/pts \
--volume /dev/null:/dev/ptmx \
--volume /sys/fs/selinux \
--volume /var/log/journal \
--volume /run/user/1000:/run/user/1000:rslave \
--volume /etc/hosts:/etc/hosts:ro \
--volume /etc/resolv.conf:/etc/resolv.conf:ro \
--volume /etc/hostname:/etc/hostname:ro \
--annotation run.oci.keep_original_groups=1 \
--ulimit host \
--userns keep-id \
--entrypoint /usr/bin/entrypoint \
docker.io/library/alpine \
--verbose \
--name "asdf" \
--user 1000 \
--group 1000 \
--home "/home/ben" \
--init "0" \
--nvidia "0" \
--pre-init-hooks "" \
--additional-packages "" \
-- ''
```



### Describe the results you received

`podman system check` considers my container, a layer, and image all damaged.

Removing the newly created container does not repair the damage.

```
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 1f3e46996e29 done   | 
Copying config b0c9d60fc5 done   | 
Writing manifest to image destination
43b37915adb73d2bdf3de0ebc5d1622f6029486f3dcb197d2f21e163f8d610f3
ben@localhost:~$ podman system check
Damaged layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc:
4 errors occurred:
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /bin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /sbin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /usr/bin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /usr/sbin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified

Damaged image b0c9d60fc5e3fa2319a86ccc1cdf34c94c7e69766e8cebfb4111f7e54f39e8ff:
4 errors occurred:
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /bin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /sbin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /usr/bin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /usr/sbin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified

Damaged container 43b37915adb73d2bdf3de0ebc5d1622f6029486f3dcb197d2f21e163f8d610f3:
4 errors occurred:
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /bin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /sbin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /usr/bin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /usr/sbin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified

Error: damage detected in local storage
ben@localhost:~$ 

```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected that `podman create` would create a container that doesn't generate warnings from `podman system check`
I also expected that deleting the container would clean up issues, but `podman system check` still flags the image.

```
ben@localhost:~$ podman container rm alpine 
alpine
ben@localhost:~$ podman system check
Damaged layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc:
4 errors occurred:
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /bin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /sbin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /usr/bin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /usr/sbin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified

Damaged image b0c9d60fc5e3fa2319a86ccc1cdf34c94c7e69766e8cebfb4111f7e54f39e8ff:
4 errors occurred:
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /bin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /sbin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /usr/bin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified
	* layer 94c88b4c188a65f15f3e03b5dc2b047b4a10f9821730b965e64ffd2a81044fbc: /usr/sbin(mtime:0x677e5bda￫0x6791c520), layer content modified

Error: damage detected in local storage
ben@localhost:~$ 
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Fresh Fedora 41 Server install into a virtual machine.
Podman 5.3.1 (the newest in Fedora right now, including rawhide)

### Additional information

If I issue a `podman pull docker.io/library/alpine` and run `podman system check`, I do not receive any error messages. Further, if I `podman run` with that image, creating a container, that container also passes the system check without complaint. 

I have also seen this mtime error with bootc images, without running a create. This could be a separate issue, the mtime is set to 0 in those, they are built with an `rpm-ostree image create` process.

```
podman pull quay.io/fedora/fedora-bootc
podman system check

....

	* layer d5a1ac8fd472fca920f7c6cfc15f3776c42600bf395995732ba7c9af6d5d9646: /usr/lib/python3.13/site-packages/botocore/vendored/requests/packages/urllib3/__pycache__(mtime:0x0￫0x6791c367), layer content modified
	* layer d5a1ac8fd472fca920f7c6cfc15f3776c42600bf395995732ba7c9af6d5d9646: /usr/share(mtime:0x0￫0x6791c367), layer content modified
	* layer d5a1ac8fd472fca920f7c6cfc15f3776c42600bf395995732ba7c9af6d5d9646: /usr/share/doc(mtime:0x0￫0x6791c367), layer content modified
	* layer d5a1ac8fd472fca920f7c6cfc15f3776c42600bf395995732ba7c9af6d5d9646: /usr/share/doc/python3-botocore(mtime:0x0￫0x6791c367), layer content modified

Error: damage detected in local storage
```

comments.body: you observe this on a plain fedora system (not fcos)?

> you observe this on a plain fedora system (not fcos)?

Yes, in my report I state that I can reproduce this on a new Fedora Server 41 installation from iso image into a vm.
Once in the section "Steps to reproduce" and once in the section "Additional environment details"

All of the output I included is from Fedora 41 Server.

Hello, I’m observing the same thing on a Fedora Silverblue 43: on a fresh Podman system (after a `podman system reset`), I use `podman build` to create a custom toolbox container image and then use `toolbox enter` to create and enter my toolbox container. Using `podman system check`, I see two damaged layers with several `layer content modified` lines.

The first damaged layer comes from the custom toolbox container image:
```
Damaged layer 96edbf7cdc3e2c48945b74fbd43e7532f64e2634bc0b8f09e43d8a0993bc4d47:
1 error occurred:
	* layer 96edbf7cdc3e2c48945b74fbd43e7532f64e2634bc0b8f09e43d8a0993bc4d47: /etc(mtime:0x68ecf144￫0x68ecf1f9), layer content modified
```
```
podman images
REPOSITORY                                 TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED         SIZE
localhost/fedora-toolbox                   43          eb22b7d14daa  20 minutes ago  5.85 GB
registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-toolbox  43          8c18ea866950  5 hours ago     1.09 GB
```

$ podman inspect eb22b7d14daa | grep 96edbf7cdc3e2c48945b74fbd43e7532f64e2634bc0b8f09e43d8a0993bc4d47

```

```

…

```
